project: a daily generative art practice, after Zach Lieberman
batch_note: 008 — diez estudios de un partido · tercera curaduría 06·07.

sketches.title: órbitas
sketches.note: The goal as gravity: 38 shots in orbit, on-target tighter, the five goals spiral in and ring the bell.

sketches.title: minutos
sketches.note: The match told only in type: 120 minutes tick by, goal numerals slam full-width and stack into the ledger.

sketches.title: sismógrafo
sketches.note: 120 stacked seismic traces, one per minute; shots tremble, goals spike off the chart.

sketches.title: mosaico
sketches.note: A sidewalk mosaic read like a clock: each glazed shard coloured by who held that minute, gold shards at the five goals — the own goal unglazed. Assembled ring by ring.

sketches.title: tapiz
sketches.note: The match hand-knotted as a pampa tapestry: guarda-pampa bands at the goal minutes, carved relief that catches the light, yarn twist, abrash, fringe — woven once on a bare warp.

sketches.title: papel
sketches.note: Torn-paper collage: five hand-torn shapes (the own goal torn as a ring), 38 confetti shots, white torn rims and real shadows. Placed once, then pressed flat.

sketches.title: terreno
sketches.note: A topographic paper relief of match pressure: shots as hills, goals as mountains, contours in ink.

sketches.title: vitral
sketches.note: The match as stained glass: leaded panes split 64/36, five rose-window roundels at the goal minutes — the own goal left clear. The light rises once, then the window glows.
Every person in the stadium, one gpu point each — 64,478 souls leaning with the match; goals ripple through the crowd.

sketches.title: bruma
sketches.note: The match as weather: five soft gradient forms — the own goal a hollow ring — drift in, settle, and rest. The dither lives in the pigment.
